intitule: Exercise 1: Identify the Gerund or Infinitive

enonce.texte:
Read each sentence below. Identify whether the underlined word or phrase is a gerund (verb + -ing used as a noun) or an infinitive (to + base verb). Then, state its function (subject, object, or complement).

Example: Swimming is good for health. → Gerund, subject.

questions.texte: She enjoys <u>reading</u> novels in her free time.
His only wish is <u>to travel</u> around the world.
<u>Recycling</u> plastic bottles reduces waste in the environment.
The government decided <u>to ban</u> single-use plastic bags.

enonce.texte: Complete each sentence with the correct gerund form of the verb in parentheses. Pay attention to the preposition.

questions.texte: Many young people are addicted to ______ (smoke) cigarettes despite the health risks.
Instead of ______ (throw) away perishable food, we should donate it to those in need.
She is interested in ______ (become) a doctor to fight diseases like HIV/AIDS and tuberculosis.
They apologized for ______ (arrive) late to the community meeting about deforestation.

enonce.texte:
Rewrite each sentence using the infinitive of purpose (to + verb) to explain why someone does something.

Example: She went to the market. She wanted to buy fresh vegetables. → She went to the market to buy fresh vegetables.

questions.texte: Many citizens vote during elections. They want to choose their representatives.
questions.reponse: Many citizens vote during elections to choose their representatives.

questions.texte: The government planted thousands of trees. It wanted to stop global warming and climate change.
questions.reponse: The government planted thousands of trees to stop global warming and climate change.

questions.texte: Parents teach their children about tolerance and integration. They want to promote living together in peace.
questions.reponse: Parents teach their children about tolerance and integration to promote living together in peace.

enonce.texte:
You are a youth leader in your Cameroonian community. You are organizing a campaign to fight against drug addiction among teenagers. Write a short paragraph (4-6 sentences) using at least three gerunds and three infinitives of purpose. Use the vocabulary from Lesson 8 (Health and Body) and Lesson 19 (Family and Community).

Your paragraph should explain:
1. What activities you will organize (gerunds as objects or subjects).
2. Why you are doing those activities (infinitives of purpose).

questions.reponse: Example answer: Organizing awareness workshops in schools is essential to prevent drug addiction. We will focus on educating teenagers about the dangers of smoking and alcohol abuse. Our team plans to invite former addicts to share their experiences. We are creating sports activities to keep young people busy after school. Parents and elders are encouraged to participate in discussing solutions with their children. Working together as a community will help us to build a healthier future for our youth.

sousQuestions.reponse: organizing, educating, discussing, working (or others from the student's paragraph).

sousQuestions.texte: List the infinitives of purpose you used.
sousQuestions.reponse: to prevent, to share, to keep, to build (or others from the student's paragraph).

intitule: Exercise 5: Real-life Decision Making – Choosing a Leader

enonce.texte:
Read the situation below. Then answer the questions using gerunds, infinitives of purpose, and gerunds after prepositions.

Situation: Your school is electing a new student council president. Two candidates have made promises:

  • Candidate A: "I will focus on improving the school canteen menu (balanced diet, rice, fish, vegetables). I believe in listening to all students' opinions."
  • Candidate B: "My goal is to reduce bullying and violence in the schoolyard. I am committed to creating a safe environment for everyone."

questions.texte: Identify one gerund used as a subject in Candidate A's statement.
questions.reponse: There is no gerund as subject in Candidate A's statement directly. 'improving' is object of preposition 'on'. 'listening' is object of preposition 'in'. (Accept if student notes none; or they can restate: 'Improving the canteen is important' – but that's not in the quote.)

questions.texte: Rewrite Candidate B's goal using an infinitive of purpose.
questions.reponse: Candidate B wants to reduce bullying and violence to create a safe environment for everyone.

questions.texte: You are a voter. Explain your choice using the structure: 'Instead of voting for X, I prefer voting for Y because...' and include a gerund after a preposition.
questions.reponse: Example: Instead of voting for Candidate A, I prefer voting for Candidate B because I believe in stopping violence before improving the menu. / Another: Instead of voting for Candidate B, I prefer voting for Candidate A because I am interested in eating healthy food every day.

questions.texte: Write one sentence advising the loser about what they should avoid doing in the future, using 'avoid + gerund'.
questions.reponse: Example: You should avoid making promises you cannot keep. / You should avoid ignoring the problem of bullying.
